title: ProjectAnalysis
description: The complete structured analysis Claude produces for a project. Every top-level section carries parallel technical and layman text variants so the HTML report can switch views without a network round-trip.

$schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Increment on breaking field changes. Consumers gate on this value.

projectName.description: Human-readable project name — e.g. the repository folder name or the name field from the top-level manifest.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when Claude produced this analysis.

detectedStack.description: The humanName of the detected primary stack (e.g. 'Node.js / JavaScript'). Set to 'Unknown' when no manifest matched. For multi-stack monorepos, join with ' + ' (e.g. 'Node.js / JavaScript + Python').

overview.description: High-level summary of what the project is and does.

tech_stack.description: Breakdown of every language, framework, tool, and runtime detected in the project.

technical.description: Technical narrative of the full technology stack.

layman.description: Plain-English explanation of the technology stack for a non-developer.

entries.description: Individual stack entries, one per language/framework/tool.

role.description: What this technology does, e.g. 'primary language'.

version.description: Version string from manifest, or 'unknown' if not pinned.

architecture.description: Structural overview of how the project is organized and how its major parts connect.

technical.description: Technical narrative: layers, modules, data flows, service boundaries.

layman.description: Plain-English analogy of how the pieces fit together.

mermaid.description: Valid Mermaid diagram source, or empty string when no meaningful diagram can be produced. Renderer shows 'No diagram available' on empty string.

algorithms.description: Key algorithms or data-processing patterns used in the project.

entries.description: May be empty for projects with no notable algorithms.

methodologies.description: Development methodologies, workflows, and process patterns observed.

pattern_rationale.description: Explains why the observed patterns were chosen — reasoning, not just names.

pitfalls.description: Known risks, anti-patterns, technical debt, and gotchas.

entries.description: At least one required. For pristine projects, use a low-severity note.

severity.description: Renderer color-codes low=yellow, medium=orange, high=red. InteractiveJS defaults to 'medium' on invalid value.

glossary.description: Definitions for key terms. Rendered as hover-tooltips and a dedicated section. May be empty.

technical.description: Optional. Precise technical definition. Omit when no nuance beyond layman.

quiz.description: Multiple-choice questions testing understanding of this project. May be empty.

answerIndex.description: 0-based index into choices. Must be < choices.length — JSON Schema cannot enforce this cross-field constraint; InteractiveJS validates at runtime.

technical.description: Technical prose for the power-user view.

layman.description: Plain-English prose for the beginner view.
